---
title: "Catcher Framing"
author: "Morgan Corliss"
date: 04/28/2026
format: pdf
---

Gen AI Usage Statement: This notebook was produced with the aid of Claude Code in RStudio

```{r}
#library() + load cached RDS (commented out)

library(baseballr)
library(httr)
library(tidyverse)

# ── Load cached data if already pulled (comment out to re-run) ─────────────
# sc_2025_raw      <- readRDS("statcast_2025_raw.rds")
# bootstrap_framing <- readRDS("bootstrap_framing_50.rds")
```

```{r}
#build_savant_url + windows + pull_savant_chunk functions

# Pull data from Baseball Savant (skip if loading from RDS above) 
build_savant_url <- function(start, end) {
  paste0(
    "https://baseballsavant.mlb.com/statcast_search/csv?all=true",
    "&player_type=pitcher",
    "&game_date_gt=", start,
    "&game_date_lt=", end,
    "&type=details",
    "&hfSea=2025%7C",
    "&game_type=R"
  )
}

# 3-day windows
breaks <- seq(as.Date("2025-03-27"), as.Date("2025-09-28"), by = "3 days")

windows <- tibble(
  start = breaks,
  end   = c(breaks[-1] - 1, as.Date("2025-09-28"))
)

# Check how many chunks we'll be making
nrow(windows)  # should be ~62 chunks

pull_savant_chunk <- function(start, end, retries = 3) {
  url <- build_savant_url(start, end)
  tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv")
  
  for (i in seq_len(retries)) {
    resp <- tryCatch(
      GET(url, write_disk(tmp, overwrite = TRUE),
          timeout(300),
          add_headers(
            `User-Agent`      = "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36",
            `Accept`          = "text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8",
            `Accept-Language` = "en-US,en;q=0.5"
          )),
      error = function(e) { message("Attempt ", i, " failed: ", e$message); NULL }
    )
    
    if (!is.null(resp) && status_code(resp) == 200) {
      df <- read_csv(tmp, show_col_types = FALSE)
      if ("pitch_type" %in% names(df)) {
        message("Got ", nrow(df), " pitches: ", start, " – ", end)
        return(df)
      }
    }
    message("Retrying in 10s...")
    Sys.sleep(10)
  }
  warning("All retries failed for ", start, " to ", end)
  return(NULL)
}
```
```{r}
#test pull

test <- pull_savant_chunk("2025-03-27", "2025-03-29")
nrow(test)  # should be well under 25,000
```

```{r}
#full season pull

sc_2025_raw <- pmap_dfr(windows, function(start, end) {
  pull_savant_chunk(as.character(start), as.character(end))
}) %>%
  mutate(game_date = as.Date(game_date, origin = "1970-01-01"))
```

```{r}
#date fix + sanity check + saveRDS

# Fix date column, verify, and save
sc_2025_raw <- sc_2025_raw %>%
  mutate(game_date = as.Date(game_date, origin = "1970-01-01"))

cat("Total rows:", nrow(sc_2025_raw), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(sc_2025_raw), "\n")
cat("Date range:", as.character(min(sc_2025_raw$game_date)),
    "to", as.character(max(sc_2025_raw$game_date)), "\n")
table(sc_2025_raw$description) |> sort(decreasing = TRUE) |> head(10)

saveRDS(sc_2025_raw, "statcast_2025_raw.rds")
```


```{r}
#shadow_threshold definition

#https://www.mlb.com/glossary/statcast/catcher-framing

#Catcher Framing Runs converts a catcher’s called strikes to runs saved on a .125 runs per strike basis.


# Shadow zone threshold derived from MLB official sources:
# Definition: one baseball width inside and outside the strike zone
# Source: https://www.mlb.com/glossary/statcast/catcher-framing
#
# Baseball diameter from official circumference spec (pg 17):
# Source: https://mktg.mlbstatic.com/mlb/official-information/2026-official-baseball-rules.pdf
# Circumference: 9.000 to 9.250 inches
# Diameter min: 9.000 / pi = 2.8648 inches = 0.23873 ft
# Diameter max: 9.250 / pi = 2.9444 inches = 0.24537 ft
# Diameter avg: (0.23873 + 0.24537) / 2 = 0.24205 ft

shadow_threshold <- ((9.000 / pi) + (9.250 / pi)) / 2 / 12
```

```{r}
#shadow_2025 filter

shadow_2025 <- sc_2025_raw %>%
  filter(
    description %in% c("called_strike", "ball"),
    !is.na(plate_x), !is.na(plate_z),
    !is.na(sz_top),  !is.na(sz_bot),
    !is.na(fielder_2)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    is_strike = as.integer(description == "called_strike"),
    pz_norm   = (plate_z - sz_bot) / (sz_top - sz_bot),
    dist_x    = pmin(abs(plate_x - 0.83), abs(plate_x + 0.83)),
    dist_zt   = abs(plate_z - sz_top),
    dist_zb   = abs(plate_z - sz_bot),
    dist_edge = pmin(dist_x, dist_zt, dist_zb),
    in_shadow = dist_edge <= shadow_threshold
  ) %>%
  filter(in_shadow) %>%
  mutate(
    catcher_id = as.factor(fielder_2),
    pitcher_id = as.factor(pitcher),
    batter_id  = as.factor(batter),
    count      = as.factor(paste0(balls, "-", strikes)),
    stand      = as.factor(stand),
    p_throws   = as.factor(p_throws)
  ) %>%
  select(
    game_date, game_pk,
    pitcher_id, batter_id, catcher_id,
    is_strike, plate_x, plate_z, pz_norm,
    sz_top, sz_bot, dist_edge,
    count, stand, p_throws
  )
```

```{r}
#shadow zone sanity checks

cat("Shadow zone pitches:", nrow(shadow_2025), "\n")
cat("Unique catchers:", n_distinct(shadow_2025$catcher_id), "\n")
cat("Strike rate in shadow zone:", round(mean(shadow_2025$is_strike), 3), "\n")
```

```{r}
#pitch distribution per catcher

shadow_2025 %>%
  count(catcher_id, name = "n_pitches") %>%
  summarise(
    min    = min(n_pitches),
    p25    = quantile(n_pitches, 0.25),
    median = median(n_pitches),
    p75    = quantile(n_pitches, 0.75),
    max    = max(n_pitches)
  )

# Also see how many catchers survive a 200-pitch cutoff
shadow_2025 %>%
  count(catcher_id) %>%
  filter(n >= 200) %>%
  nrow()
```

```{r}
#eligible_catchers + shadow_model

eligible_catchers <- shadow_2025 %>%
  count(catcher_id) %>%
  filter(n >= 300) %>%
  pull(catcher_id)

shadow_model <- shadow_2025 %>%
  filter(catcher_id %in% eligible_catchers) %>%
  mutate(catcher_id = droplevels(catcher_id))

cat("Pitches in model dataset:", nrow(shadow_model), "\n")
cat("Catchers in model:", n_distinct(shadow_model$catcher_id), "\n")
```

```{r}
library(lme4)

framing_model <- glmer(
  is_strike ~ plate_x + pz_norm + count + stand + p_throws +
              (1 | catcher_id) + (1 | pitcher_id) + (1 | batter_id),
  data   = shadow_model,
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                         optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)

summary(framing_model)
```
```{r}
#framing_model_reduced, AIC + LRT

# ── Fit reduced model (catcher random effect only) ─────────────────────────
framing_model_reduced <- glmer(
  is_strike ~ plate_x + pz_norm + count + stand + p_throws +
              (1 | catcher_id),
  data    = shadow_model,
  family  = binomial(link = "logit"),
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                         optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))
)

# ── Compare via AIC ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
AIC(framing_model, framing_model_reduced)

# ── Likelihood ratio test ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
anova(framing_model_reduced, framing_model, test = "Chisq")
```
```{r}
library(knitr)

model_comparison_table <- tibble(
  Model            = c("Full model", "Reduced model"),
  `Random effects` = c("Catcher + Pitcher + Batter", "Catcher only"),
  AIC              = c(204944.9, 205241.2),
  `Delta AIC`      = c(0.0, 296.3),
  `p-value`        = c("—", "< 2.2e-16")
) %>%
  kable(
    format  = "simple",
    digits  = 1
  )

variance_fixed_table <- tibble(
  Term          = c(
    "Pitcher (random)",
    "Batter (random)",
    "Catcher (random)",
    "plate_x (fixed)",
    "pz_norm (fixed)",
    "count 3-0 (fixed)",
    "count 0-2 (fixed)",
    "standR (fixed)",
    "p_throwsR (fixed)"
  ),
  Estimate = c(
    "—", "—", "—",
    "-0.014", "-0.047", "0.586", "-1.897", "0.140", "-0.012"
  ),
  `Std. Dev. / Std. Error` = c(
    "0.112", "0.126", "0.077",
    "0.006", "0.010", "0.038", "0.036", "0.016", "0.017"
  ),
  `Variance` = c(
    "0.013", "0.016", "0.006",
    "—", "—", "—", "—", "—", "—"
  ),
  `p-value` = c(
    "—", "—", "—",
    "0.027", "< 2e-16", "< 2e-16", "< 2e-16", "< 2e-16", "0.468"
  )
) %>%
  kable(
    format  = "simple",
    digits  = 3
  )

```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

#heatmap

library(ggplot2)

# Create a 2D grid of called strike rate across plate location
heatmap_plot <- shadow_2025 %>%
  mutate(
    px_bin = round(plate_x / 0.1) * 0.1,
    pz_bin = round(plate_z / 0.1) * 0.1
  ) %>%
  # ADD THIS FILTER to remove bins outside the shadow zone region
  filter(
    abs(px_bin) <= (0.83 + shadow_threshold + 0.05),
    pz_bin >= (1.5 - shadow_threshold - 0.05),
    pz_bin <= (3.5 + shadow_threshold + 0.05)
  ) %>%
  group_by(px_bin, pz_bin) %>%
  summarise(
    strike_rate = mean(is_strike),
    n_pitches   = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(n_pitches >= 50) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = px_bin, y = pz_bin, fill = strike_rate)) +
  geom_tile() +
  
  # Strike zone box (average dimensions)
  annotate("rect",
           xmin = -0.83, xmax = 0.83,
           ymin = 1.5,   ymax = 3.5,
           fill = NA, color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  
  # Shadow zone boundary
  annotate("rect",
         xmin = -(0.83 + shadow_threshold), xmax = (0.83 + shadow_threshold),
         ymin = 1.5  - shadow_threshold,    ymax = 3.5  + shadow_threshold,
         fill = NA, color = "white",
         linewidth = 0.6, linetype = "dashed") +
  
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "dodgerblue",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "firebrick",
    midpoint = 0.5,
    name     = "Called strike rate"
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title    = "Called strike rate by pitch location (2025 shadow zone)",
    subtitle = "Dashed box = shadow zone boundary",
    x        = "Horizontal position (ft, catcher's perspective)",
    y        = "Vertical position (ft)"
  ) +
  
  coord_fixed(xlim = c(-1.5, 1.5), ylim = c(0.5, 4.5)) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

#pitch distribution histogram

pitchdist_plot <- shadow_2025 %>%
  count(catcher_id, name = "n_pitches") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = n_pitches)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  
  # Mark the 300 pitch cutoff
  geom_vline(xintercept = 300, color = "firebrick",
             linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = 320, y = Inf,
           label = "300 pitch\nminimum",
           hjust = 0, vjust = 1.5,
           color = "firebrick", size = 3.5) +
  
  labs(
    title    = "Shadow zone pitches per catcher (2025 season)",
    subtitle = "111 total catchers; 85 meet the 300-pitch minimum",
    x        = "Number of shadow zone pitches",
    y        = "Number of catchers"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

#strike rate by count

countrate_plot <- shadow_2025 %>%
  group_by(count) %>%
  summarise(
    strike_rate = mean(is_strike),
    n_pitches   = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) %>%
  # Order counts logically
  mutate(count = fct_reorder(count, strike_rate)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = strike_rate, y = count, fill = strike_rate)) +
  geom_col() +
  geom_text(aes(label = scales::percent(strike_rate, accuracy = 0.1)),
            hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(
    low      = "dodgerblue",
    mid      = "white",
    high     = "firebrick",
    midpoint = mean(shadow_2025$is_strike),
    guide    = "none"
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.12))
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Called strike rate by count in shadow zone (2025 season)",
    subtitle = "Batter-friendly counts show higher strike rates on borderline pitches",
    x        = "Called strike rate",
    y        = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(panel.grid.minor = element_blank())
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
#| warning: false

#strike rate by handedness

handedness_plot <- shadow_2025 %>%
  group_by(stand, count) %>%
  summarise(
    strike_rate = mean(is_strike),
    n_pitches   = n(),
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    count = fct_reorder(count, strike_rate),
    stand = recode(stand, "L" = "Left-handed", "R" = "Right-handed")
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = strike_rate, y = count, color = stand)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n_pitches), alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = count), color = "gray70", linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("Left-handed"  = "steelblue",
               "Right-handed" = "firebrick"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  scale_size_continuous(
    name   = "Pitches",
    range  = c(2, 6),
    labels = scales::comma
  ) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::percent) +
  labs(
    title    = "Called strike rate by count and batter handedness",
    subtitle = "2025 shadow zone, lines connect the same count across handedness",
    x        = "Called strike rate",
    y        = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position  = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}
# ── Extract random effects with conditional standard errors ────────────────
# Following course demo approach 
# 90% CI: estimate ± 1.645 * std.error

library(broom.mixed)

catcher_ranef <- tidy(framing_model, effects = "ran_vals") %>%
  filter(group == "catcher_id") %>%
  arrange(estimate) %>%
  mutate(
    lower = estimate - 1.645 * std.error,
    upper = estimate + 1.645 * std.error,
    level = fct_inorder(level),
    sig   = lower > 0 | upper < 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(level = as.character(level))
```


```{r}
# ── Caterpillar plot ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
caterpillar_plot <- catcher_ranef %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = estimate, y = level, color = sig)) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = lower, xmax = upper),
                 height = 0, alpha = 0.5, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_point(size = 1.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
             color = "black", linewidth = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("FALSE" = "gray50", "TRUE" = "firebrick"),
    labels = c("FALSE" = "CI includes zero",
               "TRUE"  = "Significantly different from zero"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Catcher framing random effects (2025 season)",
    subtitle = "90% confidence intervals based on conditional standard errors",
    x        = "Framing random effect (log-odds scale)",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.y        = element_blank(),
    axis.ticks.y       = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}
caterpillar_plot
```

```{r}
# ── Catcher names lookup ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(baseballr)

catcher_ids <- catcher_ranef %>%
  pull(level) %>%
  as.character() %>%
  as.numeric()

catcher_names <- map_dfr(catcher_ids, function(id) {
  tryCatch({
    info <- mlb_people(person_ids = id)
    tibble(
      level        = as.character(id),
      catcher_name = info$full_name
    )
  }, error = function(e) {
    tibble(level = as.character(id), catcher_name = as.character(id))
  })
})
```

```{r}

```

```{r}
# ── Catcher pitch counts ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
catcher_pitch_counts <- shadow_model %>%
  count(catcher_id, name = "n_pitches") %>%
  mutate(level = as.character(catcher_id))

# ── Constants ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
runs_per_strike <- 0.125

# ── Runs saved table ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
runs_saved <- catcher_ranef %>%
  left_join(catcher_pitch_counts, by = "level") %>%
  left_join(catcher_names, by = "level") %>%
  mutate(
    prob_estimate     = plogis(estimate) - 0.5,
    prob_lower        = plogis(lower)    - 0.5,
    prob_upper        = plogis(upper)    - 0.5,
    runs_saved_total  = prob_estimate * n_pitches * runs_per_strike,
    runs_lower_total  = prob_lower    * n_pitches * runs_per_strike,
    runs_upper_total  = prob_upper    * n_pitches * runs_per_strike,
    runs_saved_per100 = prob_estimate * 100 * runs_per_strike,
    runs_lower_per100 = prob_lower    * 100 * runs_per_strike,
    runs_upper_per100 = prob_upper    * 100 * runs_per_strike
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(runs_saved_total))
```

```{r}
#| echo: false
 
catcher_pitch_counts

runs_saved
```

```{r}
# ── Runs saved bar chart ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
top_bottom <- runs_saved %>%
  slice(c(1:10, (n()-9):n())) %>%
  mutate(
    catcher_name = fct_reorder(catcher_name, runs_saved_total),
    direction    = ifelse(runs_saved_total > 0, "Above average", "Below average")
  )

runssaved_plot <- top_bottom %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = runs_saved_total, y = catcher_name, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, alpha = 0.85) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = runs_lower_total, xmax = runs_upper_total),
                 height = 0.25, linewidth = 0.5, color = "gray30") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linewidth = 0.6, color = "black") +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Above average" = "steelblue",
               "Below average" = "firebrick"),
    name   = NULL
  ) +
  labs(
    title    = "Catcher framing value: top and bottom 10 (2025 season)",
    subtitle = "Total runs saved; error bars = 90% CI from conditional standard errors",
    x        = "Runs saved",
    y        = NULL
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor   = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    legend.position    = "bottom"
  )
```

```{r}
runssaved_plot
```


```{r}
# ── Leaderboard table ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
leaderboard_table <- runs_saved %>%
  select(catcher_name, n_pitches,
         runs_saved_total, runs_lower_total, runs_upper_total,
         runs_saved_per100) %>%
  rename(
    `Catcher`        = catcher_name,
    `Shadow pitches` = n_pitches,
    `Runs saved`     = runs_saved_total,
    `Lower 90% CI`   = runs_lower_total,
    `Upper 90% CI`   = runs_upper_total,
    `Runs saved/100` = runs_saved_per100
  ) %>%
  slice(c(1:10, (n()-9):n())) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), round, 2)) %>%
  kable(
    format  = "simple"
  )
```

```{r}
leaderboard_table
```

```{r}
# ── Save all report objects ────────────────────────────────────────────────
saveRDS(heatmap_plot,           "heatmap_plot.rds")
saveRDS(pitchdist_plot,         "pitchdist_plot.rds")
saveRDS(countrate_plot,         "countrate_plot.rds")
saveRDS(handedness_plot,        "handedness_plot.rds")
saveRDS(caterpillar_plot,       "caterpillar_plot.rds")
saveRDS(runssaved_plot,         "runssaved_plot.rds")
saveRDS(model_comparison_table, "model_comparison_table.rds")
saveRDS(variance_fixed_table,   "variance_fixed_table.rds")
saveRDS(leaderboard_table,      "leaderboard_table.rds")
```
